text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both academic and professional settings. The first step is good time management—break large tasks into smaller, manageable pieces and set specific goals for each day. Prioritization is also key; identify which tasks are urgent or important and focus on them first. Sometimes, you may need to adjust the scope of a project when too many demands arise; learn to say no or renegotiate deadlines if the workload becomes unrealistic. Finally, communication is essential. If you anticipate a delay, inform your manager or team early. Honest updates help everyone plan better and can often lead to solutions that reduce stress for the whole group.
